name: Propeller — Profile-Guided Linker Optimisation
summary: Google 2021: BOLT's successor in post-link layout. Rather than rewriting the final binary (BOLT's approach), Propeller produces *relinkable* object files. Layout optimisation runs during the link step using profile data. Cleaner integration with build systems; comparable gains to BOLT (3–7% typical) at lower operational overhead.

sections.title: Overview
sections.content: Google's **Propeller** (2021) is BOLT's successor in the post-link layout-optimisation space. The twist: rather than rewriting the final binary (BOLT's approach, which is hard to run in production because the binary changes between the profile run and the optimised build), Propeller produces *relinkable* object files. The optimiser runs during the link step, using profile data to guide block placement and function ordering.

sections.title: Design advantage
sections.content:
The architectural advantage: Propeller's output is a standard relinked binary, easier to integrate with existing build systems. The performance gains are comparable to BOLT — 3–7% typical — with lower operational overhead.

The message for language tooling: layout optimisation is valuable enough to be built into the linker. Designing an object-file format and linker that expose block-granularity layout control from the start is cheaper than retrofitting it later.
